title: Cyclosorus bartlettii
intro: Cyclosorus bartlettii, also known as Bartlett's fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae, native to Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus bartlettii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cyclosorus bartlettii is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various health issues, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus bartlettii is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae. This classification places it within the order Polypodiales, which is a group of ferns. The order Polypodiales is further divided into several families, with Thelypteridaceae being one of them. Within Thelypteridaceae, Cyclosorus bartlettii belongs to the genus Cyclosorus, which is a group of ferns characterized by their unique leaf morphology. The exact classification of Cyclosorus bartlettii is subject to ongoing research and refinement.
